frameworks.name: confirmation bias
frameworks.one_line: selective enhancement of choice-consistent evidence during perceptual decisions

frameworks.name: drift-diffusion model
frameworks.one_line: evidence accumulation extended to gate spatial sampling during choice formation

frameworks.name: active sensing
frameworks.one_line: overt eye movements treated as evidence-selection rather than passive viewing

synopsis.core_question: Does confirmation bias shape where observers look during perceptual decision-making, not just how they interpret evidence?
key_assumptions: saccadic target selection reflects ongoing decision state
perceptual choice formation can be captured by drift-diffusion accumulation
confirmation bias generalizes from passive (pupil) to active (oculomotor) readouts
manipulations: motion-direction discrimination of noisy random-dot stimulus
strength of accumulated choice signal varied across trials
key_findings: saccadic targets biased toward locations consistent with eventual perceptual choice
magnitude of saccadic bias scales with strength of the choice signal
drift-diffusion fit reveals interaction between choice accumulation and overt sampling
interpretations: same accumulated evidence that drives choice also gates spatial allocation of attention
confirmation bias operates not only in stimulus interpretation but in self-selected sampling
